title: Getting Started
description: Install, orient, and build the smallest complete SceneMax3D scene.

title: SceneMax Language Fundamentals
description: Variables, arrays, conditions, procedures, events, and reusable expressions.

title: Objects, Models, and Assets
description: Create primitives, load models, manage resources, sprites, pools, and object lifecycle.

title: Movement, Animation, and Replay
description: Move objects, rotate them, ease motion, play animations, and replay paths.

title: Cameras and Cinematics
description: Manual cameras, chase/attach cameras, cinematic rigs, camera systems, and modifiers.

title: Lighting and Visual Effects
description: Lights, sky, shadows, Effekseer effects, built-in effects, and visual recipes.

title: Physics, Collisions, and Gameplay Systems
description: Physics motion, collision events, weapon colliders, minimaps, audio, levels, and shared state.

title: Screen, UI, and Drawing
description: Canvas setup, skyboxes, header text, UI messages, UI easing, drawing, and material texture use.

title: IDE Designers and Authoring Tools
description: Weapons Designer, Throw Motion Designer, IK Designer, and Effekseer designer workflows.

title: AI, MCP, Plugins, and Asset Automation
description: Built-in MCP server, MCP tool reference, Meshy AI, Sketchfab, plugin system, and AI-assisted IDE workflows.

title: Packaging and Publishing
description: Release prep, itch.io integration, Butler setup, authentication, uploads, and limitations.
